title: centaurea bimorpha
intro: Centaurea bimorpha, also known as two-horned knapweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, including relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Centaurea bimorpha can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional folk medicine, although its efficacy and safety are not extensively scientifically documented.
